name: Opening spiel
scoring_notes:
Two call types:
CLIENT CALL: Score 3 = restaurant name + agent introduces with any name (我是[any name]). Score 2 = restaurant name but no self-intro. Score 1 = no greeting or wrong restaurant. '我是' audible in audio = credit the intro even if name is garbled.
CHINESE ASSISTANT CALL: Score 3 = (a) English greeting to dispatcher present, AND (b) Mandarin greeting to owner contains '老板你好/老板娘你好' AND the 食客通 brand name. Score 2 = only one of the two greeting moments present, or greeting present but 食客通 missing. Score 1 = neither present.
IMPORTANT — 食客通 pronunciation: In audio recordings, 食客通 (Shí Kè Tōng) is frequently mispronounced or garbled. Accept ANY phonetically plausible attempt: 十个通, 十客通, 食可通, 实客通, 十个通话, or any 3-syllable phrase in the greeting position that sounds like Shí Kè Tōng. If the agent says '老板娘你好，这是[anything]', the [anything] should be credited as 食客通 unless it is clearly a different phrase entirely.
score_1: No greeting at all, or wrong company/restaurant identified in either part of the call
score_2: Partial greeting — one part present but the other missing or incorrect
score_3: Correct opening for call type: (1) Client call: '[Restaurant Name]老板您好，我是[any name]' — restaurant name + agent self-introduction; (2) Chinese assistant call: English greeting to dispatcher ('Hi how can I help you?') AND Mandarin greeting to owner with '老板你好/老板娘你好' + 食客通 brand name

name: Tone, no jargon & dead air
scoring_notes: Flag unmanaged dead air (>5 sec silence with no verbal update). Jargon includes internal system names or acronyms said to the client. Minor filler words (um, ah) are acceptable.
score_1: Abrupt, dismissive, heavy jargon, or long unmanaged silences
score_2: Mostly professional; minor jargon or brief dead air
score_3: Warm, composed, plain language; silence managed with verbal updates

name: Empathy & composure
scoring_notes: If no escalation or complaint moment occurred, default to 3. Only penalise if the agent was visibly defensive, dismissive, or failed to acknowledge the client's frustration.
score_1: Defensive or dismissive on a difficult call
score_3: Consistent empathy; de-escalated naturally. Mark 3 if no difficult moment arose.

name: Mandarin proficiency
scoring_notes: For CHINESE ASSISTANT CALLS: The call alternates between English (with dispatcher) and Mandarin (with restaurant owner/client). Only assess Mandarin proficiency for the segments where the agent is speaking Mandarin TO the client. The English dispatcher segments should be completely ignored for this criterion — they are expected and correct. If the entire call is in English (English-speaking client), mark 3 automatically. The ONLY accepted English loanwords in Mandarin speech are: APP, OK, lunch, combo, menu, hi, bye, hello. Any other English — self delivery, driver, third-party, dish names in English — scores 2.
score_1: Primarily English or significant language mismatch in the Mandarin-client portion
score_2: Any English beyond the accepted list (APP, OK, lunch, combo, menu, hi, bye, hello) used while speaking with a Mandarin client
score_3: Full Mandarin throughout client-facing portions; only accepted loanwords used (APP, OK, lunch, combo, menu, hi, bye, hello). For Chinese assistant calls, score only the Mandarin outbound-to-owner segment — the English dispatcher segments are expected and not penalised.

name: Listening & concise questioning
scoring_notes: Penalise if the agent asked the same question twice, or proceeded to a solution before confirming key details (restaurant ID, order number, customer name).
score_1: Proceeded without basic info OR excessive re-probing
score_2: Got key facts; some unnecessary questions or missed follow-ups
score_3: All relevant info gathered efficiently; no redundant questions

name: Root cause triage
scoring_notes: Penalise wrong triage even if the call happened to resolve correctly. A lucky resolution after wrong diagnosis is still a score of 2.
score_1: Wrong diagnosis; resolved the wrong issue entirely
score_2: Identified the issue but misread the root cause
score_3: Accurate root cause; correct solution path chosen

name: Knowledge application
scoring_notes: Note whether the knowledge gap was product (menu, pricing) or process (escalation path, SLA). Score 1 only for clear misunderstanding, not for edge cases.
score_2: General knowledge; gaps on edge cases or procedures
score_3: Confident, accurate product and process application

name: Correct resolution / escalation
scoring_notes: A correct resolution with no timeline given = score 2. Escalation is acceptable if the reason and expected timeline are communicated clearly.
score_1: Wrong resolution given OR escalated unnecessarily
score_2: Correct decision but vague timeline or incomplete execution
score_3: Resolved correctly OR escalated with clear reason and timeline

name: Ticket documentation
scoring_notes: WIRA ticket data is provided alongside the call for automated scoring. If tickets exist: score 3 = agent clearly documented intent/actions matching the ticket(s); score 2 = ticket exists but intent type or actions seem incomplete; score 1 = ticket exists but agent clearly failed to document. If NO ticket data is available and ticket actions were not mentioned in the recording: set score to null — cannot assess.
score_1: No ticket created OR completely wrong intent type
score_2: Ticket created; minor intent or sub-intent error
score_3: Correct intent, sub-intent, and clear case notes

name: Ownership
scoring_notes: Score 1 only if the agent transferred or ended the call without attempting any resolution. Borderline escalations with partial effort = 2.
score_1: Passed to another team without attempting resolution
score_2: Attempted resolution; escalated but borderline necessary
score_3: Resolved independently OR escalated only where truly required

name: CMA self-serve coaching
scoring_notes: Self-serve coaching is applicable whenever the issue could be resolved by the restaurant owner in CMA next time (menu edits, hours, settings). If not applicable, mark 3.
score_1: Missed opportunity entirely — no CMA mention on an applicable call
score_2: Mentioned CMA self-serve but did not walk client through steps
score_3: Proactively coached client through specific CMA steps; confirmed they can self-serve next time

name: Clear close & confirmed understanding
scoring_notes: A clear close requires: summary of what was resolved, and confirmation the client understands next steps. Missing either = score 2.
score_1: Abrupt end; client unclear on resolution or next steps
score_2: Adequate close but no confirmation of client understanding
score_3: Clear resolution summary; confirmed client understanding before closing
